prompt: This class is supposed to enforce that Temperature values stay above absolute zero (-273.15), but the check never triggers. What is the bug?
choices: validate() is private and cannot be called from the constructor
The set() method bypasses validate(); it should call validate() after updating the field
celsius should be an int, not a double, for comparisons to work correctly
The constructor should call set() instead of assigning the field directly
explanation: validate() is called in the constructor, so initial values are clamped. But set() assigns celsius directly without calling validate(), so any call to set() can bypass the constraint. Fix: add validate(); at the end of the set() method.
